relevant_files.rationale: Current single source of truth for blind-box category content and the four-bucket runtime data shape.
UI labels, input parsing, extraction order, history keys, and output formatting all depend on the four-bucket shape.
Some state modifiers conflict with the new no-reflection and no-transparency quality rules.
Contains local maintenance constraints and the current direction for the blind-box library rewrite.
Tracks repository-level direction and must stay aligned with the accepted content model.

existing_patterns: The blind-box library is stored as static Python dictionaries.
Each current category exposes name, large, medium, small, and hanging lists.
The UI uses category_info to bind runtime buckets to controls and labels.
The input format supports numeric box ids and per-bucket overrides.
draw_history.json applies cycle and cooldown bias but does not understand semantic item layers.
Optional item states are added by randomly combining a small set of prefix groups.
architecture_constraints: Short-term work must preserve compatibility with large, medium, small, and hanging.
Current input parsing expects numeric ids, so compatibility strategy matters.
draw_history item keys are tied to box id and bucket key.
The data layer should remain data-only, without new UI or persistence logic.
The project remains a local tkinter tool, not a service.
integration_points: Game content extraction/内容抽取.py category_info
content_risks: Legacy category ids and history keys can drift if new categories are introduced without mapping.
Fixed-size bucket authoring encourages filler items and repeated container patterns.
The hanging bucket conflicts with the new direction to isolate condition-heavy items.
Item state modifiers can reintroduce reflection and transparency language.
The current extraction logic does not understand the new five-layer semantics.
There is no automated quality validation for blind-box data.
recommended_spec_focus: define mapping back to the four runtime buckets
pilot three categories before full expansion
document quality gates and risky-item isolation
